---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r,results='asis'}
# Load required packages
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Load data
df <- read_csv("sectores_all.csv")

# Parse dates
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    fecha_start = as.Date(fecha_start),
    fecha_end   = as.Date(fecha_end)
  )

# Standardize portfolio names for display
df <- df %>%
  mutate(cartera_label = case_when(
    cartera == "PCM"       ~ "Premier",
    cartera == "MEF"       ~ "Economy",
    cartera == "Interior"  ~ "Interior",
    cartera == "Educacion" ~ "Education",
    cartera == "salud"     ~ "Health",
    TRUE ~ cartera
  ))

# Build summary table
spell_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(cartera_label) %>%
  summarise(
    Spells    = n(),
    From      = format(min(fecha_start, na.rm = TRUE), "%Y"),
    To        = format(max(fecha_end,   na.rm = TRUE), "%Y"),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Coverage = paste0(From, "--", To)) %>%
  select(Portfolio = cartera_label, Spells, Coverage) %>%
  # Fix display order
  arrange(match(Portfolio, 
    c("Premier", "Economy", "Interior", "Education", "Health")))

# Add total row
total_row <- tibble(
  Portfolio = "\\textbf{Total}",
  Spells    = sum(spell_summary$Spells),
  Coverage  = paste0(
    min(df$fecha_start, na.rm = TRUE) %>% format("%Y"),
    "--",
    max(df$fecha_end,   na.rm = TRUE) %>% format("%Y")
  )
)

spell_summary <- bind_rows(spell_summary, total_row)

# Add rationale column manually
rationale <- c(
  "Coordinates cabinet action and sustains executive viability.",
  "Shapes fiscal policy, macroeconomic management, and investment capacity.",
  "Addresses public order, internal security, and coercive capacity.",
  "Governs one of the most persistent citizen-facing public policy challenges.",
  "Governs a central welfare portfolio linked to citizens' everyday experience of state capacity.",
  "Five portfolios central to executive coordination, fiscal capacity, public order, education, and welfare."
)

spell_summary <- spell_summary %>%
  mutate(Rationale = rationale)

# Produce LaTeX table
cat(spell_summary %>%
  kbl(
    format    = "latex",
    booktabs  = TRUE,
    escape    = FALSE,
    caption   = "Ministerial Spells by Portfolio",
    label     = "spells_portfolio",
    col.names = c("Portfolio", "Spells", "Coverage", "Rationale for Inclusion")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"),
    font_size     = 9
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "3cm") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "1.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(3, width = "2.5cm") %>%
  column_spec(4, width = "7cm") %>%
  row_spec(nrow(spell_summary), bold = TRUE) %>%
  footnote(
    general = "Source: Author's ministerial-spell database.",
    general_title = "",
    escape = FALSE
  ))
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
library(ggsurvfit)

# Load and prepare data
df <- read_csv("sectores_all.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    fecha_start = as.Date(fecha_start),
    fecha_end   = as.Date(fecha_end),
    duration    = as.numeric(fecha_end - fecha_start)
  ) %>%
  # Assign epochs by start date
  mutate(epoch = case_when(
    year(fecha_start) <= 1883 ~ "1_State_Formation",
    year(fecha_start) <= 1933 ~ "2_Oligarchic",
    year(fecha_start) <= 1975 ~ "3_Power_Sharing",
    year(fecha_start) <= 1993 ~ "4_Transition",
    TRUE                      ~ "5_Democracy"
  )) %>%
  # Standardize portfolio names
  mutate(portfolio = case_when(
    cartera == "PCM"       ~ "Premier",
    cartera == "MEF"       ~ "Economy",
    cartera == "Interior"  ~ "Interior",
    cartera == "Educacion" ~ "Education",
    cartera == "salud"     ~ "Health",
    TRUE ~ cartera
  )) %>%
  # Event indicator: 1 = spell ended (observed exit)
  # All spells in historical data are complete except 
  # those still running in 2026
  mutate(
    event = if_else(fecha_end >= as.Date("2026-01-01"), 0L, 1L)
  ) %>%
  # Remove zero-duration spells
  filter(duration > 0, !is.na(duration))

# Create survival object
surv_obj <- Surv(time = df$duration, event = df$event)

# Quick check
summary(df$duration)
table(df$epoch)
table(df$event)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

desc_epoch <- df %>%
  group_by(epoch) %>%
  summarise(
    N      = n(),
    Median = median(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean   = round(mean(duration, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    SD     = round(sd(duration, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    Min    = min(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max    = max(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(epoch = c(
    "State Formation",
    "Oligarchic State",
    "Power-Sharing State",
    "Transition",
    "Democracy w/o Parties"
  ))

cat(
  desc_epoch %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Ministerial Duration by Institutional Epoch (days)",
      label     = "desc_epoch",
      col.names = c("Epoch","N","Median","Mean","SD","Min","Max")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position","scale_down"),
      font_size = 9
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "4cm") %>%
    footnote(
      general = "Duration in days. Source: Author's ministerial-spell database.",
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```
```{r}
desc_portfolio <- df %>%
  group_by(portfolio) %>%
  summarise(
    N      = n(),
    Median = median(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    Mean   = round(mean(duration, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    SD     = round(sd(duration, na.rm = TRUE), 0),
    Min    = min(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max    = max(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(match(portfolio,
    c("Premier","Economy","Interior","Education","Health")))

cat(
  desc_portfolio %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Ministerial Duration by Portfolio (days)",
      label     = "desc_portfolio",
      col.names = c("Portfolio","N","Median","Mean","SD","Min","Max")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position","scale_down"),
      font_size = 9
    ) %>%
    footnote(
      general = "Duration in days. Source: Author's ministerial-spell database.",
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```

```{r}
crosstab <- df %>%
  group_by(epoch, portfolio) %>%
  summarise(
    median_dur = median(duration, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = portfolio,
    values_from = median_dur
  ) %>%
  mutate(epoch = c(
    "State Formation",
    "Oligarchic State",
    "Power-Sharing State",
    "Transition",
    "Democracy w/o Parties"
  )) %>%
  select(epoch, Premier, Economy, Interior, Education, Health)

cat(
  crosstab %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Median Duration by Epoch and Portfolio (days)",
      label     = "crosstab_epoch_portfolio",
      col.names = c("Epoch","Premier","Economy",
                    "Interior","Education","Health")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position","scale_down"),
      font_size = 9
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "4cm") %>%
    footnote(
      general = "Median duration in days. NA indicates no spells for that combination. Source: Author's ministerial-spell database.",
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```


#4.3
```{r}
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(patchwork)
library(tidyverse)

# ============================================================
# Shared theme settings
# ============================================================

surv_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.text       = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90"),
    plot.title        = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title        = element_text(size = 10)
  )

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 1 — KM by epoch
# ============================================================

km_epoch <- survfit(surv_obj ~ epoch, data = df)

p1 <- ggsurvplot(
  km_epoch,
  data        = df,
  palette     = c("#d62728","#ff7f0e","#2ca02c","#1f77b4","#9467bd"),
  legend.labs = c(
    "State Formation (1824-1883)",
    "Oligarchic State (1883-1933)",
    "Power-Sharing State (1933-1975)",
    "Transition (1975-1993)",
    "Democracy w/o Parties (1993-2022)"
  ),
  legend.title = "",
  xlab        = "Time in Office",
  ylab        = "Probability of Remaining in Office",
  xlim        = c(0, 1500),
  break.x.by  = 365,
  xscale      = 1,
  axes.offset = FALSE,
  ggtheme     = surv_theme,
  legend      = "bottom",
  size        = 0.8,
  conf.int    = FALSE,
  risk.table  = FALSE
)$plot +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1500),
    breaks = c(0, 365, 730, 1095, 1460),
    labels = c("0", "1yr", "2yr", "3yr", "4yr")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(ncol = 2))

ggsave("km_epoch.pdf", plot = p1,
       width = 7, height = 5, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("km_epoch.png", plot = p1,
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
print(p1)

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 2 — KM by portfolio
# ============================================================

km_portfolio <- survfit(surv_obj ~ portfolio, data = df)

p2 <- ggsurvplot(
  km_portfolio,
  data        = df,
  palette     = c("#d62728","#2ca02c","#9467bd","#ff7f0e","#1f77b4"),
  legend.labs = c("Economy","Education","Health","Interior","Premier"),
  legend.title = "",
  xlab        = "Time in Office",
  ylab        = "Probability of Remaining in Office",
  xlim        = c(0, 1500),
  break.x.by  = 365,
  axes.offset = FALSE,
  ggtheme     = surv_theme,
  legend      = "bottom",
  size        = 0.8,
  conf.int    = FALSE,
  risk.table  = FALSE
)$plot +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1500),
    breaks = c(0, 365, 730, 1095, 1460),
    labels = c("0", "1yr", "2yr", "3yr", "4yr")
  )

ggsave("km_portfolio.pdf", plot = p2,
       width = 7, height = 5, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("km_portfolio.png", plot = p2,
       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
print(p2)

# ============================================================
# FIGURE 3 — Economy vs Interior by epoch (belt test)
# ============================================================

df_belt <- df %>%
  filter(portfolio %in% c("Economy", "Interior")) %>%
  mutate(
    epoch_label = factor(recode(epoch,
      "1_State_Formation" = "State Formation",
      "2_Oligarchic"      = "Oligarchic State",
      "3_Power_Sharing"   = "Power-Sharing State",
      "4_Transition"      = "Transition",
      "5_Democracy"       = "Democracy w/o Parties"
    ), levels = c(
      "State Formation",
      "Oligarchic State",
      "Power-Sharing State",
      "Transition",
      "Democracy w/o Parties"
    ))
  )

plots <- lapply(
  levels(df_belt$epoch_label), function(e) {
    sub <- df_belt %>% filter(epoch_label == e)
    fit <- survfit(Surv(duration, event) ~ portfolio, data = sub)
    ggsurvplot(
      fit,
      data        = sub,
      palette     = c("#d62728", "#ff7f0e"),
      legend.labs = c("Economy", "Interior"),
      legend.title = "",
      title       = e,
      xlab        = "Time in Office",
      ylab        = "Prob. Remaining",
      xlim        = c(0, 1500),
      break.x.by  = 365,
      axes.offset = FALSE,
      ggtheme     = surv_theme,
      legend      = "bottom",
      size        = 0.8,
      conf.int    = FALSE,
      font.title  = c(9, "bold"),
      font.x      = 8,
      font.y      = 8,
      font.tickslab = 7
    )$plot +
      scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(0, 1500),
        breaks = c(0, 365, 730),
        labels = c("0", "1yr", "2yr")
      )
  }
)

p3 <- (plots[[1]] | plots[[2]] | plots[[3]]) /
      (plots[[4]] | plots[[5]] | plot_spacer()) +
  plot_annotation(
    caption = "Economy vs. Interior ministerial survival by institutional epoch."
  )

ggsave("km_belt.pdf", plot = p3,
       width = 12, height = 8, device = cairo_pdf)
ggsave("km_belt.png", plot = p3,
       width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)
print(p3)
```


```{r}
library(kableExtra)

lr_table <- data.frame(
  Epoch    = c(
    "State Formation",
    "Oligarchic State",
    "Power-Sharing State",
    "Transition",
    "Democracy w/o Parties"
  ),
  N        = as.numeric(logrank_epoch$n),
  Observed = as.numeric(logrank_epoch$obs),
  Expected = round(as.numeric(logrank_epoch$exp), 1),
  OminusE  = round(as.numeric(logrank_epoch$obs) -
                   as.numeric(logrank_epoch$exp), 1)
)

cat(
  lr_table %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Log-Rank Test of Survival Differences Across Institutional Epochs",
      label     = "logrank_epoch",
      col.names = c("Epoch", "N", "Observed", "Expected", "O $-$ E")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position"),
      font_size     = 10
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "4.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(2, width = "1cm") %>%
    column_spec(3, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(4, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(5, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    kableExtra::footnote(
      general = c(
        "Log-rank test: $\\\\chi^2$ = 82.3, df = 4, $p$ $<$ 0.001.",
        "O $-$ E: observed minus expected exits under equal survival.",
        "Negative values indicate fewer exits than expected (better continuity conditions)."
      ),
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```
```{r}
# Ensure factors are set correctly
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    epoch = factor(epoch, levels = c(
      "1_State_Formation",
      "2_Oligarchic",
      "3_Power_Sharing",
      "4_Transition",
      "5_Democracy"
    )),
    portfolio = factor(portfolio, levels = c(
      "Interior",
      "Premier",
      "Economy",
      "Education",
      "Health"
    ))
  )

# Recreate survival object
surv_obj <- Surv(time = df$duration, event = df$event)

# Rerun all models
cox1 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ epoch,             data = df)
cox2 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ portfolio,          data = df)
cox3 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ epoch + portfolio,  data = df)
cox4 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ epoch * portfolio,  data = df)

# Confirm
summary(cox1)
```



```{r}
library(broom)
library(kableExtra)

# Tidy models
m1_tidy <- tidy(cox1, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(model = "Model 1")
m3_tidy <- tidy(cox3, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(model = "Model 3")

cox_table <- bind_rows(m1_tidy, m3_tidy) %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term,
      "epoch2_Oligarchic"     = "Oligarchic State",
      "epoch3_Power_Sharing"  = "Power-Sharing State",
      "epoch4_Transition"     = "Transition",
      "epoch5_Democracy"      = "Democracy w/o Parties",
      "portfolioPremier"      = "Premier",
      "portfolioEconomy"      = "Economy",
      "portfolioEducation"    = "Education",
      "portfolioHealth"       = "Health"
    ),
    HR = round(estimate, 3),
    CI = paste0("[", round(conf.low, 3), ", ",
                     round(conf.high, 3), "]"),
    p  = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "$<$0.001***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ paste0(round(p.value, 3), "**"),
      p.value < 0.05  ~ paste0(round(p.value, 3), "*"),
      TRUE            ~ as.character(round(p.value, 3))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(model, term, HR, CI, p)

# Split and combine with headers
m1_out <- cox_table %>%
  filter(model == "Model 1") %>%
  select(-model)

m3_out <- cox_table %>%
  filter(model == "Model 3") %>%
  select(-model)

combined <- bind_rows(
  tibble(term = "\\textit{Model 1: Epoch only}",
         HR = NA, CI = "", p = ""),
  m1_out,
  tibble(term = "\\textit{Model 3: Epoch + Portfolio}",
         HR = NA, CI = "", p = ""),
  m3_out
) %>%
  mutate(HR = ifelse(is.na(HR), "", as.character(HR)))

cat(
  combined %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Cox Proportional Hazards Models of Ministerial Exit",
      label     = "cox_models",
      col.names = c("", "HR", "95\\% CI", "$p$")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position"),
      font_size     = 10
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "5.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(2, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(3, width = "3.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(4, width = "2cm") %>%
    kableExtra::footnote(
      general = c(
        "Hazard ratios (HR) from Cox proportional hazards models.",
        "HR $<$ 1 indicates lower exit risk relative to the reference category.",
        "Reference categories: State Formation (epoch), Interior (portfolio).",
        "* $p$ $<$ 0.05, ** $p$ $<$ 0.01, *** $p$ $<$ 0.001.",
        "N = 1,062 spells, 1,050 events."
      ),
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```


# next
```{r}
logrank_portfolio <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ portfolio, data = df)

lr_port <- data.frame(
  Portfolio = c("Interior", "Premier", "Economy",
                "Education", "Health"),
  N         = as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$n),
  Observed  = as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$obs),
  Expected  = round(as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$exp), 1),
  OminusE   = round(as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$obs) -
                    as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$exp), 1)
)



cat(
  lr_port %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Log-Rank Test of Survival Differences Across Portfolios",
      label     = "logrank_portfolio",
      col.names = c("Portfolio","N","Observed","Expected","O $-$ E")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position"),
      font_size     = 10
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "3.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(2, width = "1cm") %>%
    column_spec(3, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(4, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(5, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    kableExtra::footnote(
      general = c(
        "Log-rank test: $\\\\chi^2$ = 21.1, df = 4, $p$ = 0.0003.",
        "O $-$ E: observed minus expected exits under equal survival.",
        "Negative values indicate fewer exits than expected."
      ),
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```


```{r}
data.frame(
  names = names(logrank_portfolio$n),
  N     = as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$n),
  Obs   = as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$obs),
  Exp   = round(as.numeric(logrank_portfolio$exp), 1)
)
```




```{r}
library(broom)
library(kableExtra)

m4_tidy <- tidy(cox4, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)

# Extract only Economy interactions
belt_table <- m4_tidy %>%
  filter(grepl("Economy", term)) %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term,
      "portfolioEconomy"                    = "Economy (main effect)",
      "epoch2_Oligarchic:portfolioEconomy"  = "Economy × Oligarchic State",
      "epoch3_Power_Sharing:portfolioEconomy" = "Economy × Power-Sharing State",
      "epoch4_Transition:portfolioEconomy"  = "Economy × Transition",
      "epoch5_Democracy:portfolioEconomy"   = "Economy × Democracy w/o Parties"
    ),
    HR = round(estimate, 3),
    CI = paste0("[", round(conf.low, 3), ", ",
                     round(conf.high, 3), "]"),
    p  = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "$<$0.001***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ paste0(round(p.value, 3), "**"),
      p.value < 0.05  ~ paste0(round(p.value, 3), "*"),
      TRUE            ~ as.character(round(p.value, 3))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(term, HR, CI, p)

cat(
  belt_table %>%
    kbl(
      format    = "latex",
      booktabs  = TRUE,
      escape    = FALSE,
      caption   = "Cox Model 4: Economy Portfolio Effects by Epoch (Protective Belt Test)",
      label     = "cox_belt",
      col.names = c("Term", "HR", "95\\% CI", "$p$")
    ) %>%
    kable_styling(
      latex_options = c("hold_position"),
      font_size     = 10
    ) %>%
    column_spec(1, width = "6cm") %>%
    column_spec(2, width = "1.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(3, width = "3.5cm") %>%
    column_spec(4, width = "2cm") %>%
    kableExtra::footnote(
      general = c(
        "Hazard ratios from Cox Model 4 (epoch $\\\\times$ portfolio interaction).",
        "Reference categories: State Formation (epoch), Interior (portfolio).",
        "HR $<$ 1 indicates lower exit risk relative to Interior in State Formation.",
        "* $p$ $<$ 0.05, ** $p$ $<$ 0.01, *** $p$ $<$ 0.001.",
        "N = 1,062 spells, 1,050 events."
      ),
      general_title = "",
      escape = FALSE
    )
)
```

















```{r}
# KM by epoch
km_epoch <- survfit(surv_obj ~ epoch, data = df)

# Log-rank test
logrank_epoch <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ epoch, data = df)
print(logrank_epoch)

# Plot
ggsurvfit(km_epoch) +
  labs(
    title    = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Institutional Epoch",
    x        = "Days in Office",
    y        = "Probability of Remaining in Office",
    color    = "Epoch"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "1_State_Formation" = "#d62728",
      "2_Oligarchic"      = "#ff7f0e",
      "3_Power_Sharing"   = "#2ca02c",
      "4_Transition"      = "#1f77b4",
      "5_Democracy"       = "#9467bd"
    ),
    labels = c(
      "1_State_Formation" = "State Formation (1824-1883)",
      "2_Oligarchic"      = "Oligarchic State (1883-1933)",
      "3_Power_Sharing"   = "Power-Sharing State (1933-1975)",
      "4_Transition"      = "Transition (1975-1993)",
      "5_Democracy"       = "Democracy w/o Parties (1993-2022)"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# KM by portfolio
km_portfolio <- survfit(surv_obj ~ portfolio, data = df)
logrank_portfolio <- survdiff(surv_obj ~ portfolio, data = df)
print(logrank_portfolio)

ggsurvfit(km_portfolio) +
  labs(
    title = "Kaplan-Meier Survival Curves by Portfolio",
    x     = "Days in Office",
    y     = "Probability of Remaining in Office",
    color = "Portfolio"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
```{r}
# Recode epoch and portfolio as factors with reference categories
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    epoch     = factor(epoch, levels = c(
      "1_State_Formation","2_Oligarchic",
      "3_Power_Sharing","4_Transition","5_Democracy"
    )),
    portfolio = factor(portfolio, levels = c(
      "Interior","Premier","Economy","Education","Health"
    ))
  )

# Model 1: epoch only
cox1 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ epoch, data = df)
summary(cox1)

# Model 2: portfolio only
cox2 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ portfolio, data = df)
summary(cox2)

# Model 3: epoch + portfolio (main model)
cox3 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ epoch + portfolio, data = df)
summary(cox3)

# Model 4: interaction epoch x portfolio (belt test)
cox4 <- coxph(surv_obj ~ epoch * portfolio, data = df)
summary(cox4)

# Compare models
anova(cox1, cox3, test = "LRT")
anova(cox2, cox3, test = "LRT")
anova(cox3, cox4, test = "LRT")
```
